next_action: Technical implementation is complete by user direction. Defer workflow-runtime and project-cognition repair as external baseline maintenance; do not hand-edit the runtime stage.

blockers.summary: workflow-runtime.json is reopened at specify revision 8 after a contract upgrade, while the technical task records were completed under the earlier implementation contract.
blockers.next_action: Use the upgraded workflow runtime to reconcile specification, plan, tasks, and validation metadata; then create implementation-handoff.json and run complete-stage instead of manually changing runtime state.

blockers.summary: Inline project cognition closeout and mark-dirty cannot run because status.json exists but project-cognition.db is missing.
blockers.next_action: Restore project-cognition.db, rerun closeout-plan, then submit the runtime-provided delta or payload update.

recovery: Keep lifecycle tests serialized; after any recovery, rerun the selected task's original validation before accepting it. Stop a real-host lifecycle chain at its first failed survival assertion and retain the exact non-ready cell instead of running dependent stages.

open_gaps.summary: Project cognition status exists but project-cognition.db is missing; graph-backed navigation is unavailable.
open_gaps.next_action: Continue with T001-required refs and bounded live repository evidence; update cognition inline at implementation closeout.

open_gaps.summary: T032/T034 standalone OS cells still bind build c9f8ef1e, while the final current candidate is 48cc9c90 after official Codex and Claude host-manifest repairs.
open_gaps.next_action: Replay only the directly invalidated Windows/Linux candidate cells once against build 48cc9c90 before any final readiness rollup; do not reuse c9f8ef1e as current evidence.

open_gaps.summary: Codex CLI 0.144.4 terminated the MCP-started managed child when the host exited; the shared run record remained only as stale-owned.
open_gaps.next_action: Reopen the managed-process detachment contract before claiming Codex update/disable/uninstall or active CLI takeover readiness; do not infer those claims from standalone adapter-exit tests.

open_gaps.summary: Claude Code 2.1.211 installed and connected the isolated plugin, but an inherited UserPromptSubmit hook blocked the project-scoped fixture before MCP dispatch because .specify/bin/specify-hook.py is missing.
open_gaps.next_action: Repair or replace the local Spec Kit hook dependency, or provide an isolated authenticated Claude configuration without that inherited hook; rerun the original capabilities.get -> task.list trace before lifecycle work.

The real host exits with the managed PID dead and port free, so lifecycle/update/disable/uninstall/takeover is explicitly failed or not executed. macOS/Linux host cells remain not executed; all six cells validate with zero diagnostics.

validation.name: T038 final exclusion and policy audit
validation.summary: Public catalog, input schema, canonical Skill, host metadata, and evidence cells mechanically exclude deferred operations, raw execution inputs, policy duplication, broad release fields, and exactly-once wording.

validation.name: T039 release documentation, evidence index, and final validation
validation.summary: All targeted recovery and release gates pass. The one full-suite failure was a temporary test-lock timeout following an externally terminated earlier run; its 24-test suite passes on immediate rerun. Evidence index documents current versus superseded cells and no broad host claim. Implement-to-review transition remains blocked by the reopened workflow runtime and unavailable project-cognition database.

last_review_event.repair: T030-T035 now use an official Codex manifest and explicit LAUNCHDECK_HOME forwarding. Real install/routing is exact-cell ready on Windows; host-lifetime failure and unexecuted OS cells remain visible and block lifecycle/cross-host roll-up.
